headline: Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill Defeated in Lok Sabha
detail: The Bill aimed to decouple women's 33% reservation (106th Amendment) from a post-2026 Census by allowing use of 2011 Census data for delimitation (amending Article 82), and proposed expanding Lok Sabha from 550 to 850 seats. It received 298 votes vs the required 352 two-thirds majority. Southern states opposed seat expansion due to demographic disadvantage.
tags: Women Reservation, 131st Amendment, Delimitation, Article 82, 106th Amendment, Lok Sabha seats
mcq: The Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill 2026 proposed amending which Article to enable delimitation based on 2011 Census? → Article 82

headline: India Hosts 16th BRICS NSA Meeting in New Delhi (June 22–23)
detail: India, as BRICS Chair 2026, hosted the National Security Advisers' conclave chaired by NSA Ajit Doval. Theme: 'Non-traditional security challenges confronting the world today'. Delegates included China's Wang Yi and Russia's NSA Sergei Shoigu. Discussions covered cyber threats, AI-driven risks, counter-terrorism. The meet prepares ground for the BRICS Summit in September 2026 in India.
tags: BRICS 2026, BRICS Chair India, NSA Meeting, Ajit Doval, Non-traditional security, Cyber threats
mcq: India is BRICS Chair in 2026. The theme of the BRICS NSA Meeting held in June 2026 was? → Non-traditional security challenges confronting the world today

headline: India's FY2026 GDP at 7.7%; RBI Keeps Repo Rate at 5.25%
detail: India's real GDP grew 7.7% in FY2026 (revised up from 7.6%), making it the fastest-growing G20 economy. RBI's MPC held the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% (SDF: 5.00%; MSF: 5.50%). Forex reserves stand at $682.3 billion (~11 months import cover). FY2027 GDP projected at 6.6%. CPI inflation projected at 5.1% for FY2027; crude oil at ~$110/barrel poses upside risk.
tags: GDP 7.7%, Repo Rate 5.25%, Forex Reserves $682B, RBI MPC, CPI Inflation, India Economy
mcq: India's Forex reserves as of May 29, 2026 stood at? → $682.3 billion (approximately 11 months import cover)

headline: India-UAE in Talks for ~$4 Billion BrahMos & Akashteer Deal
detail: UAE is in early talks to buy BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles and Akashteer (fully automated air defence C2 system by BEL/Indian Army) from India — a potential $4 billion deal. India's defence exports hit ₹33,000+ crore in FY2026 (from ₹60 crore in 2013-14). BrahMos debuted in combat during the 2025 India-Pakistan conflict; deals with Vietnam and Indonesia already signed.
tags: BrahMos, Akashteer, BEL, Defence Exports, UAE, India-Pakistan 2025, Operation Sindoor, ₹33000 crore
mcq: Akashteer, recently in news for export deals, is developed by which Indian entity? → Bharat Electronics Ltd (BEL) in partnership with Indian Army

headline: Western Ghats ESA Notification Imminent After 12-Year Delay
detail: The Central Government is finalising the Ecologically Sensitive Area (ESA) notification for the Western Ghats, 12 years after the first draft. The Ghats are one of 8 global biodiversity hotspots and a UNESCO World Heritage Site; home to 30% of world's Asian elephants and 17% of tigers. ~245 million people depend on its rivers (Godavari, Krishna, Cauvery). ESA notification regulates mining, quarrying, construction.
tags: Western Ghats, ESA, Eco-Sensitive Zone, Kasturirangan, Biodiversity Hotspot, UNESCO WHS, Nilgiri tahr
mcq: The Kasturirangan Committee (2013) was associated with which environmental issue? → Western Ghats Ecologically Sensitive Area (ESA) boundary delineation

headline: ISRO & Jal Shakti Sign MoU for Satellite-Based Water Management
detail: ISRO and the Ministry of Jal Shakti are signing an MoU to use satellite remote sensing for water resource management across 24 priority areas. This supports Jal Jeevan Mission (tap water to rural households) and Atal Bhujal Yojana (groundwater management). Separately, Aditya-L1 (India's solar observatory) has released 27 TB of solar observation data to the global public domain.
tags: ISRO, Jal Shakti, MoU, Water Management, Remote Sensing, Jal Jeevan Mission, Aditya-L1
mcq: Which ministry collaborated with ISRO for satellite-based water management across 24 priority areas in June 2026? → Ministry of Jal Shakti

headline: TRISHNA: ISRO–France (CNES) Joint Thermal Imaging Satellite Launched
detail: TRISHNA (Thermal infraRed Imaging Satellite for High-resolution Natural resource Assessment) is a joint 2026 mission of ISRO and CNES (France). It monitors crop water stress and urban heat islands using high-resolution thermal infrared imagery — critical for food security planning and climate adaptation. Earlier ISRO-CNES collaboration includes Megha-Tropiques (2011).
tags: TRISHNA, ISRO, CNES, France, Thermal Infrared, Crop Water Stress, Urban Heat Island, Megha-Tropiques
mcq: TRISHNA, a joint satellite mission of ISRO and which country's space agency, monitors crop water stress and urban heat islands? → France (CNES — Centre National d'Études Spatiales)

headline: EAM Jaishankar Visits Mongolia; Focus on Energy & Connectivity
detail: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar visited Mongolia on June 22–23, meeting its leadership for talks on energy, infrastructure, and strategic connectivity. Mongolia is landlocked between Russia and China with uranium and coal reserves — significant for India's energy security and Act East Policy. India follows a 'Third Neighbour Policy' with Mongolia.
tags: Mongolia, Jaishankar, Act East Policy, Third Neighbour Policy, Extended Neighbourhood, Energy Security, Uranium
mcq: India's 'Third Neighbour Policy' is specifically associated with which country? → Mongolia

headline: G7 Summit 2026 at Évian, France — Ukraine, Trade, AI Agenda
detail: The G7 Summit (June 15-17, Évian-les-Bains, France) reaffirmed support for Ukraine's sovereignty and welcomed the Global Convergence for Growth Summit (with China). Divergences persisted on US tariffs, NATO commitments, Middle East stance, and AI governance. The G7 'managed rather than resolved' differences. France is the 2026 G7 Chair.
tags: G7 2026, Évian, France, G7 Chair France, Ukraine, NATO, AI Governance, US Tariffs, Global Imbalances
mcq: The G7 Summit 2026 was hosted at which location in France? → Évian-les-Bains

headline: IMF Projects 3.1% Global Growth for 2026; World Bank Cuts to 2.5%
detail: IMF's WEO April 2026 (titled 'Global Economy in the Shadow of War') projects global growth at 3.1% for 2026, with global headline inflation at 4.4%. World Bank cut its 2026 forecast to 2.5% (risk scenario: 1.3%) due to the Middle East war and energy shocks (~19% crude price rise). EMDE growth at post-pandemic low of 3.6%; MENA+Pakistan region cut by 2.7pp to 1.6%.
tags: IMF, World Bank, Global Growth 2026, Middle East War, EMDE, Strait of Hormuz, WEO, Energy Shock
mcq: The IMF's April 2026 World Economic Outlook was titled? → 'Global Economy in the Shadow of War' — projected 3.1% global growth for 2026

headline: Ashtalakshmi Growth Model: NE India's 8 States as Growth Engines
detail: The 'Ashtalakshmi' (Eight Lakshmi) model coined by PM Modi refers to India's 8 NE states (Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Tripura, Sikkim) being positioned as growth engines via infrastructure, Act East connectivity, and hydropower. The model is gaining international attention as a framework for integrating border regions into national development.
tags: Ashtalakshmi, North East India, 8 NE States, Act East Policy, LPAI, Kaladan Multi-Modal, PM Modi
mcq: The 'Ashtalakshmi' model refers to the development of how many North-Eastern states? → 8 (Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Tripura, Sikkim)
